zh.lifeSummary: 當Cecily Axtell於1435年出生在英國賀特福爾貝可哈姆斯泰德時，她的父親Henry Thomas Axtell20歲，母親Joan Gould20歲。她於大約1455年與Sir William Gitton在英國英格蘭維爾爾結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。她去世於1495年在英國賀特福爾享年60歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Cecily Axtell于1435年出生在英国賀特福爾貝可哈姆斯泰德时，她的父亲Henry Thomas Axtell20岁，母亲Joan Gould20岁。她于大约1455年与Sir William Gitton在英国英格兰維爾爾结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。她去世于1495年在英国賀特福爾享年60岁。

nameMeanings.name: Axtell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: from the Middle English personal name Asketill, from Old Norse Ásketill, composed of the elements áss ‘god’ + ketill ‘sacrificial cauldron, helmet’ (see also Haskell ). This name was in use both among Scandinavian settlers in eastern England (esp. Norfolk) and among the Normans.

nickname for a salt merchant or seller of salt, from Middle English hakke-salt ‘chop salt (into pieces)’. The development of the surname to Axtel is evidenced in 16th-century Hertfordshire and Buckinghamshire.

variants.name: Axton
meanings: habitational name from Axton in Kent, named from the Old English personal name Acca + Old English stān ‘stone’.

(Buckinghamshire): possibly a variant of Axtell , or alternatively a habitational name from a metathesized form of a placename such as Ashton in Bainton, Northamptonshire, which is recorded as Axton in 1253.

variants.name: Antell
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Anglo-Norman French personal name Antil, a shortened form of Anketil, a common Norman variant of Old Norse Ásketill (see Axtell ).
English: variant of   Antill  , a habitational name from Ampthill in Bedfordshire.

Swedish and Finnish (of Swedish origin): ornamental name from placenames in Finland and Sweden such as Ant(t)ila and Antby, or from the Finnish personal name Antti (a vernacular form of Andreas ), + the suffix -ell, a derivative of the Latin adjectival ending -elius.

variants.name: Estell
meanings: variant of Eskell, from the Middle English personal name Askell, Old Norse Áskell, a shortened form of Ásketill (see Axtell ). The personal name was also popular among the Normans, sometimes in the form Anskell (ancient Germanic Ans- substituted for As-), which largely explains why the name occurs in post-Conquest England in counties not settled by Vikings.

topographic name from Middle English est ‘east’ + hal(le) ‘chief building, hall’ or hale ‘nook, remote place’ (Old English ēast + hall or halh), denoting someone who lived or worked at the ‘east hall’ or who lived at a place known as the ‘east nook’.

variants.name: Haskell
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Askell with prosthetic H-, from Old Norse Áskell, a shortened form of Ásketill (see Axtell ). The personal name was also popular among the Normans, sometimes in the form Anskell (with ancient Germanic Ans- substituted for As-), which explains why the name occurs in post-Conquest England in counties not settled by Vikings. Its appearance as a surname is often disguised by altered pronunciations; metathesis of /sk/ to /ks/, spelled -x-, for example, has given rise to Axtell . The Normans frequently used the name in Frankish forms; see Askin , Haskin , Hasty , and Astin . For the name in the Isle of Man see Castell .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): from the personal name Khaskl, a Yiddish form of the Hebrew name Yeḥezqel (see Ezekiel ).

nameMeanings.name: Cecily
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval vernacular English form of   Cecilia .

variants.name: Cecilie
meanings: Medieval vernacular English form of   Cecilia .

variants.name: Cicely
meanings: Variant of   Cecily .  This was a common form of the name in the Middle Ages, and in the 20th century was well known as the name of the British actress and singer  Cicely Courtneidge  (  1893–1980 ).

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Cecily Axtell was born in 1435, in Berkhamsted, Hertfordshire, England, her father, Henry Thomas Axtell, was 20 and her mother, Joan Gould, was 20. She married Sir William Gitton about 1455, in Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. She died in 1495, in Hertfordshire, England, at the age of 60.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Cecily Axtell est née en 1435, à Berkhamsted, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, son père, Henry Thomas Axtell, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Joan Gould, avait 20 ans. Elle a épousé Sir William Gitton environ 1455, à Wiltshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1495, à Hertfordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 60 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Cecily Axtell 1435 in Berkhamsted, Hertfordshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Henry Thomas Axtell 20 und ihre Mutter Joan Gould 20. Sie hat um 1455 in Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom Sir William Gitton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1495 im Alter von 60 Jahren in Hertfordshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Cecily Axtell è nata il nel 1435 nel Berkhamsted, Hertfordshire, England, sua padre, Henry Thomas Axtell, aveva 20 anni e sua madre, Joan Gould, aveva 20 anni. Lei sposato Sir William Gitton circa 1455, nel Wiltshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel 1495, nel Hertfordshire, England, all'età di 60 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Cecily Axtell(이)가 1435년 영국 허트퍼드실르 버카스테드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Henry Thomas Axtell(은)는 20세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan Gould(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 대략 1455년 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르에서 Sir William Gitton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1495년 영국 허트퍼드실르에서 60세의 나이로 사망했다.

Ela casou-se com Sir William Gitton aproximadamente 1455, em Wiltshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 1495, em Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, com 60 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Cecily Axtell родилась в 1435 г. в местности бирхамстед, хиртфордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Henry Thomas Axtell было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan Gould, 20. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir William Gitton около 1455 г. в местности уилцхир, Англия, Великобритания. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1495 г. в местности хиртфордшир, Англия в возрасте 60 лет.
